CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-24-2015 Continued To Mar-09-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-02-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. GENE V. HALAVANAU, TEL. NO. 1-415-692-5301 SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DOUGLAS C. STRAUS OF ARCHER NORRIS LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-925-930-6600 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT UNION BANK, N.A.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF COMPLAINT - Union Bank's motion to strike the punitive damages request is granted. Plaintiffs have not alleged any ultimate facts showing malice, fraud or oppression nor have they alleged any ultimate facts showing that any intentional misconduct by Union Bank was done, authorized or ratified by an officer, director or managing agent of Union Bank. Plaintiff was given 20 days leave to amend to allege malice. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-02-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. GENE V. HALAVANAU, TEL. NO. 1-415-692-5301 SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DOUGLAS C. STRAUS OF ARCHER NORRIS LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-925-930-6600 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT UNION BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - Union Bank's demurrer to all three claims is overruled. All three claims adequately allege that, at the time of the levy, there would have been money in the accounts levied on but for Union Bank's unilateral decision to transfer monies from those accounts to another account. These facts are sufficient to support each of plaintiffs' claims. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/hk

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-10-2015 Continued To Jan-13-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
